:root {
  --bprogress-color: var(--accent);
  --bprogress-height: 3px;
  --bprogress-spinner-size: 18px;
  --bprogress-spinner-animation-duration: 400ms;
  --bprogress-spinner-border-size: 2px;
  --bprogress-box-shadow: 0 0 10px var(--accent), 0 0 5px var(--accent);
  --bprogress-z-index: 99999;
  --bprogress-spinner-top: 15px;
  --bprogress-spinner-bottom: auto;
  --bprogress-spinner-right: 15px;
  --bprogress-spinner-left: auto;
}

.bprogress {
  width: 0;
  height: 0;
  pointer-events: none;
  z-index: var(--bprogress-z-index);
}

.bprogress .bar {
  background: var(--bprogress-color);
  position: fixed;
  z-index: var(--bprogress-z-index);
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: var(--bprogress-height);
}

/* Fancy blur effect */
.bprogress .peg {
  display: block;
  position: absolute;
  right: 0;
  width: 100px;
  height: 100%;
  box-shadow: var(--bprogress-box-shadow);
  opacity: 1.0;
  transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
}

/* Remove these to get rid of the spinner */
.bprogress .spinner {
  display: block;
  position: fixed;
  z-index: var(--bprogress-z-index);
  top: var(--bprogress-spinner-top);
  bottom: var(--bprogress-spinner-bottom);
  right: var(--bprogress-spinner-right);
  left: var(--bprogress-spinner-left);
}

.bprogress .spinner-icon {
  width: var(--bprogress-spinner-size);
  height: var(--bprogress-spinner-size);
  box-sizing: border-box;
  border: solid var(--bprogress-spinner-border-size) transparent;
  border-top-color: var(--bprogress-color);
  border-left-color: var(--bprogress-color);
  border-radius: 50%;
  -webkit-animation: bprogress-spinner var(--bprogress-spinner-animation-duration) linear infinite;
  animation: bprogress-spinner var(--bprogress-spinner-animation-duration) linear infinite;
}

.bprogress-custom-parent {
  overflow: hidden;
  position: relative;
}

.bprogress-custom-parent .bprogress .spinner,
.bprogress-custom-parent .bprogress .bar {
  position: absolute;
}

.bprogress .indeterminate {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: var(--bprogress-height);
  overflow: hidden;
}

.bprogress .indeterminate .inc,
.bprogress .indeterminate .dec {
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  height: 100%;
  background-color: var(--bprogress-color);
}

.bprogress .indeterminate .inc {
  animation: bprogress-indeterminate-increase 2s infinite;
}

.bprogress .indeterminate .dec {
  animation: bprogress-indeterminate-decrease 2s 0.5s infinite;
}

@-webkit-keyframes bprogress-spinner {
  0%   { -webkit-transform: rotate(0deg); transform: rotate(0deg); }
  100% { -webkit-transform: rotate(360deg); transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes bprogress-spinner {
  0%   { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes bprogress-indeterminate-increase {
  from { left: -5%; width: 5%; }
  to { left: 130%; width: 100%; }
}

@keyframes bprogress-indeterminate-decrease {
  from { left: -80%; width: 80%; }

how to create a complete photo vault interface that displays existing user images and enables reliable large file uploads using advanced multipart upload techniques.</p>
<p>This comprehensive tutorial teaches both the concepts and implementation details, providing a complete working example that demonstrates best practices for handling file uploads in production applications.</p>
<h2 id="understanding-multipart-upload" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Understanding Multipart Upload</h2>
<h3 id="what-is-multipart-upload" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">What is Multipart Upload?</h3>
<p>Imagine you need to mail a 500-page book to someone, but the postal service only accepts packages up to 50 pages. You could:</p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Regular approach</strong>: Try to force the entire book into one package (fails)</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Multipart approach</strong>: Split the book into 10 packages of 50 pages each, mail them separately, then have the recipient reassemble them</li>
</ol>
<p>Multipart upload works the same way with files. Instead of sending one massive HTTP request with your entire file, you:</p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Split</strong> the file into smaller chunks (typically 5-10MB each)</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Upload</strong> each chunk independently using signed URLs</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Combine</strong> the chunks server-side into the original file</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="why-use-multipart-upload" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Why Use Multipart Upload?</h3>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">🚀 Performance Benefits</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Chunks upload in parallel, utilizing full bandwidth</li>
<li class="">Faster overall upload times for large files</li>
<li class="">Better resource utilization</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">🔄 Reliability Improvements</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">If one chunk fails, only that chunk needs retry (not the entire file)</li>
<li class="">Network interruptions don’t restart the entire upload</li>
<li class="">Automatic retry logic for transient failures</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">📊 Better User Experience</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Granular progress tracking (chunk-by-chunk)</li>
<li class="">Users can see exactly how much has uploaded</li>
<li class="">Cancel/resume functionality</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">🏗️ Technical Advantages</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">No single request size limits (overcome HTTP timeouts)</li>
<li class="">Reduced server memory usage (chunks processed individually)</li>
<li class="">Improved error isolation and debugging</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="when-should-you-use-multipart-upload" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">When Should You Use Multipart Upload?</h3>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">✅ Use multipart upload when:</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Files are larger than 10MB</li>
<li class="">Users are on unreliable connections (mobile networks)</li>
<li class="">You need detailed progress feedback</li>
<li class="">Building production applications with user-generated content</li>
<li class="">Handling media files (images, videos, audio)</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">❌ Regular upload is fine when:</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Files are small (< 5MB)</li>
<li class="">Network is reliable and fast</li>
<li class="">Simple use cases without progress requirements</li>
<li class="">Prototypes or internal tools</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="how-multipart-upload-works-the-3-phase-flow" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">How Multipart Upload Works (The 3-Phase Flow)</h3>
<p>Understanding this flow is crucial for implementing multipart upload correctly:</p>
<div></div>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Phase 1: Create Upload Session (Setup)</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Tell the server you want to upload a file</li>
<li class="">Server creates a media record in database</li>
<li class="">Server starts a multipart upload session</li>
<li class="">You receive a <code>mediaUuid</code> and <code>uploadId</code> for tracking</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Phase 2: Upload Parts (The Heavy Lifting)</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Split your file into chunks (we’ll use 10MB chunks)</li>
<li class="">For each chunk:<!-- -->
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Request a signed URL for that specific part number</li>
<li class="">Upload the chunk directly to cloud storage using the signed URL</li>
<li class="">Save the ETag returned by storage (proof of successful upload)</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Phase 3: Complete Upload Session (Assembly)</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Tell the server all chunks are uploaded</li>
<li class="">Provide the list of ETags to prove which chunks were uploaded</li>
<li class="">Server combines chunks into the final file</li>
<li class="">File enters processing pipeline (thumbnail generation, etc.)</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="what-youll-build" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">What You’ll Build</h2>
<p>By the end of this tutorial, you’ll have a complete photo vault featuring:</p>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">📱 Photo Grid Interface</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Responsive grid that adapts to screen size</li>
<li class="">Displays existing vault images using optimized thumbnails</li>
<li class="">Infinite scroll pagination for large image collections</li>
<li class="">Loading states and graceful error handling</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">⬆️ Advanced Upload System</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Multipart upload with 10MB chunking</li>
<li class="">Real-time progress tracking with visual feedback</li>
<li class="">Automatic retry logic for failed chunks</li>
<li class="">Upload queue management (multiple files simultaneously)</li>
<li class="">File validation and error handling</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">🛡️ Production-Ready Features</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">OAuth authentication integration</li>
<li class="">Proper error boundaries and user feedback</li>
<li class="">Mobile-responsive design</li>
<li class="">Security best practices</li>
<li class="">Performance optimizations</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="learning-objectives" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Learning Objectives</h2>
<p>After completing this tutorial, you’ll understand:</p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">How multipart upload works under the hood</li>
<li class="">When and why to use multipart vs regular uploads</li>
<li class="">How to implement chunked file upload with progress tracking</li>
<li class="">OAuth authentication patterns for API access</li>
<li class="">React patterns for handling async file operations</li>
<li class="">Error handling strategies for network operations</li>
<li class="">Performance optimization techniques for file uploads</li>
</ul>
<div data-intent="note" class="fern-callout"><div class="flex items-start space-x-4"><div class="[&_svg]:size-icon-md mt-0.5 w-4"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-pin"><path d="M12 17v5"></path><path d="M9 10.76a2 2 0 0 1-1.11 1.79l-1.78.9A2 2 0 0 0 5 15.24V16a1 1 0 0 0 1 1h12a1 1 0 0 0 1-1v-.76a2 2 0 0 0-1.11-1.79l-1.78-.9A2 2 0 0 1 15 10.76V7a1 1 0 0 1 1-1 2 2 0 0 0 0-4H8a2 2 0 0 0 0 4 1 1 0 0 1 1 1z"></path></svg></div><div class="-my-4 flex-1 overflow-x-auto text-sm before:mb-4 before:block after:mt-4 after:block"><h5 class="leading-snug"></h5><h4 id="before-you-start" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Before You Start</h4><p><strong class="font-semibold">Complete the <a class="fern-mdx-link" href="/docs/introduction/quick-start">Quickstart Guide</a> first</strong> to set up OAuth authentication, environment variables, and your basic Fanvue app. This tutorial builds on that foundation and focuses specifically on multipart upload implementation.</p></div></div></div>
<h2 id="prerequisites" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Prerequisites</h2>
<p>Since you’ve completed the <a class="fern-mdx-link" href="/docs/introduction/quick-start">Quickstart Guide</a>, you should have:</p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">✅ A working Next.js app with Fanvue OAuth authentication</li>
<li class="">✅ Environment variables configured (<code>OAUTH_CLIENT_ID</code>, <code>OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET</code>, etc.)</li>
<li class="">✅ Basic <code>read:self</code> scope working for user authentication</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="additional-scopes-required" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Additional Scopes Required</h3>
<p>Add these scopes to your existing <code>OAUTH_SCOPES</code> in <code>.env.local</code>:</p>
<div class="fern-code-block not-prose bg-card-background border-card-border rounded-3 shadow-card-grayscale group relative mb-6 mt-4 flex w-full border language-env"><pre class="code-block-root <h3 id="technical-requirements" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Technical Requirements</h3>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">File API Knowledge</strong>: Understanding of JavaScript File objects and Blob handling</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">React Patterns</strong>: Experience with hooks, async state management, and component patterns</li>
</ul>
<p>Ready to dive in? Let’s start by understanding how to fetch and display existing vault images, then build up to the complete multipart upload system.</p>
<h2 id="step-1-fetch-existing-vault-images" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Step 1: Fetch Existing Vault Images</h2>
<p>Before we dive into multipart uploads, let’s start with something simpler: displaying existing images in the user’s vault. This will help us understand the API structure and build the foundation for our upload system.</p>
<h3 id="understanding-the-media-api" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Understanding the Media API</h3>
<p>The Fanvue API organizes user content into <strong class="font-semibold">media items</strong> with different <strong class="font-semibold">variants</strong> (sizes/formats). For our vault, we’ll focus on:</p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Media Type</strong>: <code>image</code> (filtering out videos and audio)</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Variant Type</strong>: <code>thumbnail_gallery</code> (optimized for grid display)</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="building-the-api-client" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Building the API Client</h3>
<p>Our API client handles OAuth authentication and provides methods for media operations. Notice how we use <strong class="font-semibold">Bearer token authentication</strong>:</p>

<p><strong class="font-semibold">Key Features Explained:</strong></p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">OAuth Authentication</strong>: Uses <code>accessToken</code> prop for secure authentication</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Responsive Grid</strong>: Adapts from 2 to 6 columns based on screen size</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Lazy Loading</strong>: Images load only when they’re about to be visible</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">State Management</strong>: Separate loading states for initial load vs. pagination</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Error Handling</strong>: Comprehensive error states with retry functionality</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Performance</strong>: Optimized with <code>useCallback</code> to prevent unnecessary re-renders</li>
</ol>
<p>Now that you understand how to display existing images, let’s move on to the exciting part: implementing multipart upload!</p>
<h2 id="step-2-implement-multipart-upload" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Step 2: Implement Multipart Upload</h2>
<p>Here’s where we get to the core of this tutorial. We’ll build a complete multipart upload system that handles large files efficiently, provides real-time progress feedback, and gracefully handles errors.</p>
<h3 id="understanding-the-upload-flow-again-but-with-code" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Understanding the Upload Flow (Again, but with Code)</h3>
<p>Before we dive into implementation, let’s revisit the 3-phase flow with more technical detail:</p>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Phase 1: Initiate Upload</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Client tells server: “I want to upload a 50MB image called ‘vacation.jpg’”</li>
<li class="">Server creates a database record for the media</li>
<li class="">Server tells cloud storage: “Prepare for a multipart upload”</li>
<li class="">Server returns: <code>{mediaUuid: "abc-123", uploadId: "xyz-789"}</code></li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Phase 2: Upload Parts</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Client splits file into 10MB chunks (5 chunks for our 50MB file)</li>
<li class="">For each chunk:<!-- -->
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Client asks: “Where do I upload chunk 3?”</li>
<li class="">Server returns a signed URL (valid for ~15 minutes)</li>
<li class="">Client uploads chunk directly to cloud storage</li>
<li class="">Storage returns an ETag (proof of successful upload)</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Phase 3: Finalize Upload</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Client tells server: “All chunks uploaded, here are the ETags”</li>
<li class="">Server tells storage: “Combine all chunks into final file”</li>
<li class="">Storage combines chunks and confirms success</li>
<li class="">Server starts processing pipeline (thumbnail generation, etc.)</li>
</ul>

<p><strong class="font-semibold">What’s happening in these upload methods:</strong></p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">createUploadSession</strong>: Creates media record and starts multipart session</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">getPartUrl</strong>: Gets signed URL for uploading a specific chunk using RESTful URL structure</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">uploadPart</strong>: Uploads chunk directly to cloud storage (bypasses our API)</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">completeUploadSession</strong>: Tells server to combine all chunks into final file using PATCH method</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="error-handling-for-upload-operations" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Error Handling for Upload Operations</h3>
<p>Network operations can fail in many ways, especially with large file uploads. Let’s add robust error handling:</p>

<p>This progress component handles all the visual feedback during upload. The key features are:</p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Status-based styling</strong>: Different colors and icons for each state</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Progress calculation</strong>: Smooth progress bar with minimum 2% width for visibility</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Interactive elements</strong>: Cancel and retry buttons when appropriate</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Educational messaging</strong>: Explains what “processing” means to users</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="building-the-main-upload-component" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Building the Main Upload Component</h3>
<p>Now comes the centerpiece: a React component that handles the complete multipart upload flow. This component coordinates file chunking, parallel uploads, progress tracking, and error recovery.</p>

<p><strong class="font-semibold">Key Implementation Details:</strong></p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Chunking Strategy</strong>: Files are split into 10MB chunks for optimal balance of reliability and performance</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Concurrency Control</strong>: Maximum 3 concurrent uploads to avoid overwhelming the server</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Progress Tracking</strong>: Real-time progress updates as chunks complete</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Error Recovery</strong>: Automatic retry for network issues and rate limits</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">User Control</strong>: Users can cancel uploads and retry failed ones</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Queue Management</strong>: Multiple files are queued and processed efficiently</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="understanding-the-upload-logic" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Understanding the Upload Logic</h3>
<p>Let’s break down the complex upload logic step by step:</p>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">File Validation</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="">Checks file type matches accepted formats</li>
<li class="">Ensures file size is within limits</li>
<li class="">Provides clear error messages for invalid files</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Upload Orchestration</strong></p>
<ol class="mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [&_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Initiate</strong>: Create media record and get upload session ID</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Chunk Processing</strong>: Split file and upload chunks in parallel batches</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Progress Tracking</strong>: Update UI as each chunk completes</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Finalization</strong>: Combine chunks server-side and start processing</li>
</ol>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Error Handling Strategy</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Network Errors</strong>: Auto-retry with exponential backoff</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Rate Limits</strong>: Wait and retry automatically</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">File Errors</strong>: Show user-friendly messages</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Cancellation</strong>: Clean up resources properly</li>
</ul>
<p>This completes our multipart upload implementation! The component handles all the complexity of chunking, uploading, and error recovery while providing a clean interface for users.</p>

<h3 id="key-architecture-decisions" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Key Architecture Decisions</h3>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Layout & UX</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Responsive Grid</strong>: 3-column layout on desktop, single column on mobile</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Progressive Enhancement</strong>: Works without JavaScript for basic functionality</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Clear Visual Hierarchy</strong>: Existing images prominent, upload panel secondary</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">State Management</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Centralized Auth</strong>: Single source of truth for user authentication</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Optimistic Updates</strong>: UI updates immediately, syncs with server</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Error Boundaries</strong>: Graceful handling of authentication and network failures</li>
</ul>
<p><strong class="font-semibold">Performance Considerations</strong></p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Lazy Loading</strong>: Images load as needed</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Suspense Boundaries</strong>: Non-blocking loading states</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Efficient Re-renders</strong>: Minimal re-renders through proper key usage</li>
</ul>

<h2 id="troubleshooting-common-issues" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Troubleshooting Common Issues</h2>
<h3 id="upload-fails-immediately" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Upload Fails Immediately</h3>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Check OAuth token</strong>: Ensure <code>read:media</code> and <code>write:media</code> scopes</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Verify file type</strong>: Only images are supported in this implementation</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Check file size</strong>: Must be under 1GB limit</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Network connectivity</strong>: Ensure API endpoint is reachable</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="progress-bar-stuck-at-0" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Progress Bar Stuck at 0%</h3>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Check initiate response</strong>: Verify mediaUuid and uploadId are received</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Check part URLs</strong>: Ensure signed URLs are being generated</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Network issues</strong>: Temporary connectivity problems</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="images-not-appearing-after-upload" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Images Not Appearing After Upload</h3>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Processing time</strong>: Large images may take minutes to process</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Status checking</strong>: Verify images reach ‘ready’ status</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Variant generation</strong>: Thumbnails must be created before display</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Refresh timing</strong>: Grid should refresh after successful upload</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="memory-issues-with-large-files" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Memory Issues with Large Files</h3>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Chunk size</strong>: Reduce from 10MB to 5MB for slower devices</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Concurrency</strong>: Reduce MAX_CONCURRENT_UPLOADS from 3 to 2</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">File cleanup</strong>: Ensure File objects are garbage collected</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="next-steps" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Next Steps</h2>
<p>This tutorial provides a solid foundation for multipart uploads. Consider extending with:</p>
<ul class="[&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Resume Capability</strong>: Store upload state in localStorage</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Background Uploads</strong>: Continue uploads when tab is hidden</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Bulk Operations</strong>: Select and delete multiple images</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Advanced Metadata</strong>: EXIF data extraction and display</li>
<li class=""><strong class="font-semibold">Image Editing</strong>: Basic crop/rotate before upload</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="production-checklist" data-state="closed" style="cursor:pointer">Production Checklist</h2>
<p>Before deploying your multipart upload vault to production:</p>
<ul class="contains-task-list [&>li]:relative [&>li]:before:text-(color:--grayscale-a10) mb-3 list-none pl-3 [&>li]:pl-3 [&>li]:before:absolute [&>li]:before:ml-[-22px] [&>li]:before:mt-[-1px] [&>li]:before:content-['⦁'] [&>li]:before:self-center">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">OAuth tokens stored securely</strong> - Never expose tokens client-side</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Error tracking implemented</strong> - Use Sentry, LogRocket, or similar</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Upload limits configured</strong> - File size, count, and rate limits</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">User feedback for all states</strong> - Loading, error, and success messaging</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Mobile-responsive design tested</strong> - Upload works on all devices</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Accessibility features added</strong> - Screen reader support, keyboard navigation</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Rate limiting handled gracefully</strong> - Exponential backoff, user messaging</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Image optimization implemented</strong> - Client-side compression where appropriate</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Content security policies configured</strong> - CSP headers to prevent XSS</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Performance monitoring</strong> - Track upload success rates and timing</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Comprehensive error logging</strong> - Log all upload failures for debugging</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled=""> <strong class="font-semibold">Backup recovery plan</strong> - Handle corrupted uploads gracefully</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p><em>This tutorial demonstrates a complete multipart upload implementation using OAuth Bearer tokens and modern React patterns. Multipart Upload?\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eImagine you need to mail a 500-page book to someone, but the postal service only accepts packages up to 50 pages. You could:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col class=\"mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [\u0026amp;_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]\"\u003e\n\u003cli class=\"\"\u003e\u003cstrong class=\"font-semibold\"\u003eRegular approach\u003c/strong\u003e: Try to force the entire book into one package (fails)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli class=\"\"\u003e\u003cstrong class=\"font-semibold\"\u003eMultipart approach\u003c/strong\u003e: Split the book into 10 packages of 50 pages each, mail them separately, then have the recipient reassemble them\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eMultipart upload works the same way with files. Instead of sending one massive HTTP request with your entire file, you:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col class=\"mb-3 list-outside list-decimal [\u0026amp;_ol]:!list-[lower-roman]\"\u003e\n\u003cli class=\"\"\u003e\u003cstrong class=\"font-semibold\"\u003eSplit\u003c/strong\u003e the file into smaller chunks (typically 5-10MB each)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli class=\"\"\u003e\u003cstrong class=\"font-semibold\"\u003eUpload\u003c/strong\u003e each chunk independently using signed URLs\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli class=\"\"\u003e\u003cstrong class=\"font-semibold\"\u003eCombine\u003c/strong\u003e the chunks server-side into the original file\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003ch3 id=\"why-use-multipart-upload\" data-state=\"closed\" Maximum size is 1GB.\", \"FILE_TOO_LARGE\");\n  }\n\n  // Rate limiting (temporary issue)\n  if (message.includes(\"429\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"rate limit\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\n      \"Too many requests. Please wait a moment before retrying.\",\n      \"RATE_LIMITED\",\n      true // This is retryable\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Authentication issues\n  if (message.includes(\"401\") || message.includes(\"403\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\"Authentication failed. Please log in again.\", \"AUTHENTICATION_FAILED\");\n  }\n\n  // Server errors (likely temporary)\n  if (message.includes(\"5\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"server error\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\n      \"Server error occurred. Please try again.\",\n      \"SERVER_ERROR\",\n      true // Server errors are usually temporary\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Generic network error (likely temporary)\n  if (message.toLowerCase().includes(\"network\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"fetch\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\n      \"Network error. Please check your connection and try again.\",\n      \"NETWORK_ERROR\",\n      true\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Unknown error\n  return new UploadError(\n    message,\n    \"UNKNOWN_ERROR\",\n    true // When in doubt, allow retry\n  );\n}\n`,className:\"language-typescript\",language:\"typescript\",lang:\"en\"})}),`\n`,(0,e.jsx)(n.h3,{id:\"building-the-upload-progress-component\",children:\"Building the Upload Progress Component\"}),`\n`,(0,e.jsx)(n.p,{children:\"Before we build the main uploader, let\\u2019s create a component that shows upload progress. Building a Photo Vault with Multipart Upload\n\nLearn how to create a complete photo vault interface that displays existing user images and enables reliable large file uploads using advanced multipart upload techniques.\n\nThis comprehensive tutorial teaches both the concepts and implementation details, providing a complete working example that demonstrates best practices for handling file uploads in production applications.\n\n## Understanding Multipart Upload\n\n### What is Multipart Upload?\n\nImagine you need to mail a 500-page book to someone, but the postal service only accepts packages up to 50 pages. You could:\n\n1. **Regular approach**: Try to force the entire book into one package (fails)\n2. **Multipart approach**: Split the book into 10 packages of 50 pages each, mail them separately, then have the recipient reassemble them\n\nMultipart upload works the same way with files. Instead of sending one massive HTTP request with your entire file, you:\n\n1. **Split** the file into smaller chunks (typically 5-10MB each)\n2. **Upload** each chunk independently using signed URLs\n3. **Combine** the chunks server-side into the original file\n\n### Why Use Multipart Upload?\n\n**🚀 Performance Benefits**\n\n* Chunks upload in parallel, utilizing full bandwidth\n* Faster overall upload times for large files\n* Better resource utilization\n\n**🔄 Reliability Improvements**\n\n* If one chunk fails, only that chunk needs retry (not the entire file)\n* Network interruptions don't restart the entire upload\n* Automatic retry logic for transient failures\n\n**📊 Better User Experience**\n\n* Granular progress tracking (chunk-by-chunk)\n* Users can see exactly how much has uploaded\n* Cancel/resume functionality\n\n**🏗️ Technical Advantages**\n\n* No single request size limits (overcome HTTP timeouts)\n* Reduced server memory usage (chunks processed individually)\n* Improved error isolation and debugging\n\n### When Should You Use Multipart Upload?\n\n**✅ Use multipart upload when:**\n\n* Files are larger than 10MB\n* Users are on unreliable connections (mobile networks)\n* You need detailed progress feedback\n* Building production applications with user-generated content\n* Handling media files (images, videos, audio)\n\n**❌ Regular upload is fine when:**\n\n* Files are small (\\\u003c 5MB)\n* Network is reliable and fast\n* Simple use cases without progress requirements\n* Prototypes or internal tools\n\n### How Multipart Upload Works (The 3-Phase Flow)\n\nUnderstanding this flow is crucial for implementing multipart upload correctly:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API\n    participant Storage\n\n    Note over Client,Storage: Phase 1: Create Upload Session\n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /media/uploads\n    API-\u003e\u003eStorage: Create multipart upload\n    Storage--\u003e\u003eAPI: Upload ID\n    API--\u003e\u003eClient: Media UUID + Upload ID\n\n    Note over Client,Storage: Phase 2: Upload Parts\n    loop For each chunk\n        Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: GET /media/uploads/{uploadId}/parts/{partNumber}/url\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: Signed URL\n        Client-\u003e\u003eStorage: PUT chunk to signed URL\n        Storage--\u003e\u003eClient: ETag for chunk\n    end\n\n    Note over Client,Storage: Phase 3: Complete Upload Session\n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: PATCH /media/uploads/{uploadId}\n    API-\u003e\u003eStorage: Complete multipart upload\n    Storage--\u003e\u003eAPI: Final file URL\n    API--\u003e\u003eClient: Processing started\n```\n\n**Phase 1: Create Upload Session (Setup)**\n\n* Tell the server you want to upload a file\n* Server creates a media record in database\n* Server starts a multipart upload session\n* You receive a `mediaUuid` and `uploadId` for tracking\n\n**Phase 2: Upload Parts (The Heavy Lifting)**\n\n* Split your file into chunks (we'll use 10MB chunks)\n* For each chunk:\n  * Request a signed URL for that specific part number\n  * Upload the chunk directly to cloud storage using the signed URL\n  * Save the ETag returned by storage (proof of successful upload)\n\n**Phase 3: Complete Upload Session (Assembly)**\n\n* Tell the server all chunks are uploaded\n* Provide the list of ETags to prove which chunks were uploaded\n* Server combines chunks into the final file\n* File enters processing pipeline (thumbnail generation, etc.)\n\n## What You'll Build\n\nBy the end of this tutorial, you'll have a complete photo vault featuring:\n\n**📱 Photo Grid Interface**\n\n* Responsive grid that adapts to screen size\n* Displays existing vault images using optimized thumbnails\n* Infinite scroll pagination for large image collections\n* Loading states and graceful error handling\n\n**⬆️ Advanced Upload System**\n\n* Multipart upload with 10MB chunking\n* Real-time progress tracking with visual feedback\n* Automatic retry logic for failed chunks\n* Upload queue management (multiple files simultaneously)\n* File validation and error handling\n\n**🛡️ Production-Ready Features**\n\n* OAuth authentication integration\n* Proper error boundaries and user feedback\n* Mobile-responsive design\n* Security best practices\n* Performance optimizations\n\n## Learning Objectives\n\nAfter completing this tutorial, you'll understand:\n\n* How multipart upload works under the hood\n* When and why to use multipart vs regular uploads\n* How to implement chunked file upload with progress tracking\n* OAuth authentication patterns for API access\n* React patterns for handling async file operations\n* Error handling strategies for network operations\n* Performance optimization techniques for file uploads\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  #### Before You Start\n\n  **Complete the [Quickstart Guide](/docs/introduction/quick-start) first** to set up OAuth authentication, environment variables, and your basic Fanvue app. This tutorial builds on that foundation and focuses specifically on multipart upload implementation.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nSince you've completed the [Quickstart Guide](/docs/introduction/quick-start), you should have:\n\n* ✅ A working Next.js app with Fanvue OAuth authentication\n* ✅ Environment variables configured (`OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`, `OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET`, etc.)\n* ✅ Basic `read:self` scope working for user authentication\n\n### Additional Scopes Required\n\nAdd these scopes to your existing `OAUTH_SCOPES` in `.env.local`:\n\n```env\n# Add these to your existing OAuth scopes from the quickstart\nOAUTH_SCOPES=read:self read:media write:media\n```\n\n\u003cNote\u003e\n  Make sure to update your app configuration in the Fanvue Developer area to include the\n  `read:media` and `write:media` scopes.\n\u003c/Note\u003e\n\n### Technical Requirements\n\n* **File API Knowledge**: Understanding of JavaScript File objects and Blob handling\n* **React Patterns**: Experience with hooks, async state management, and component patterns\n\nReady to dive in? Let's start by understanding how to fetch and display existing vault images, then build up to the complete multipart upload system.\n\n## Step 1: Fetch Existing Vault Images\n\nBefore we dive into multipart uploads, let's start with something simpler: displaying existing images in the user's vault. This will help us understand the API structure and build the foundation for our upload system.\n\n### Understanding the Media API\n\nThe Fanvue API organizes user content into **media items** with different **variants** (sizes/formats). For our vault, we'll focus on:\n\n* **Media Type**: `image` (filtering out videos and audio)\n* **Variant Type**: `thumbnail_gallery` (optimized for grid display)\n\n### Building the API Client\n\nOur API client handles OAuth authentication and provides methods for media operations. Notice how we use **Bearer token authentication**:\n\n```typescript\n// lib/fanvue-api.ts\n// Enhanced Fanvue API client with OAuth authentication\n\nconst API_BASE = \"https://api.fanvue.com\";\n\nexport interface MediaItem {\n  uuid: string;\n  status: \"created\" | \"processing\" | \"ready\" | \"error\";\n  createdAt?: string;\n  url?: string;\n  caption?: string | null;\n  description?: string | null;\n  name?: string | null;\n  variants?: Array\u003c{\n    uuid: string;\n    variantType: \"blurred\" | \"main\" | \"thumbnail\" | \"thumbnail_gallery\";\n    displayPosition: number;\n    url?: string;\n    width: number | null;\n    height: number | null;\n    lengthMs: number | null;\n  }\u003e;\n}\n\nexport interface PaginatedResponse\u003cT\u003e {\n  data: T[];\n  pagination: {\n    page: number;\n    size: number;\n    hasMore: boolean;\n  };\n}\n\nexport class FanvueAPI {\n  private accessToken: string;\n  private baseURL: string;\n\n  // 🔑 OAuth access token for authenticated requests\n  constructor(accessToken: string) {\n    this.accessToken = accessToken;\n    this.baseURL = API_BASE;\n  }\n\n  // 🛠️ Private helper method for making authenticated requests\n  private async makeRequest(endpoint: string, options: RequestInit = {}): Promise\u003cResponse\u003e {\n    const url = `${this.baseURL}${endpoint}`;\n\n    const response = await fetch(url, {\n      ...options,\n      headers: {\n        // 🔐 OAuth Bearer token authentication\n        Authorization: `Bearer ${this.accessToken}`,\n        \"X-Fanvue-API-Version\": \"2025-06-26\",\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n        ...options.headers,\n      },\n      cache: \"no-store\", // Fresh data for user's vault\n    });\n\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      let errorMessage = `HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`;\n      try {\n        const errorBody = await response.json();\n        errorMessage = errorBody.message || errorBody.error || errorMessage;\n      } catch {\n        // If we can't parse the error body, use the original message\n      }\n      throw new Error(errorMessage);\n    }\n\n    return response;\n  }\n\n  // 📸 Get user's media with flexible filtering options\n  async getUserMedia(\n    options: {\n      mediaType?: \"image\" | \"video\" | \"audio\";\n      variants?: Array\u003c\"blurred\" | \"main\" | \"thumbnail\" | \"thumbnail_gallery\"\u003e;\n      status?: Array\u003c\"created\" | \"processing\" | \"ready\" | \"error\"\u003e;\n      page?: number;\n      size?: number;\n    } = {}\n  ): Promise\u003cPaginatedResponse\u003cMediaItem\u003e\u003e {\n    // Build query parameters - the API uses URL parameters for filtering\n    const params = new URLSearchParams();\n\n    if (options.mediaType) params.set(\"mediaType\", options.mediaType);\n    if (options.variants?.length) params.set(\"variants\", options.variants.join(\",\"));\n    if (options.status?.length) params.set(\"status\", options.status.join(\",\"));\n    if (options.page) params.set(\"page\", options.page.toString());\n    if (options.size) params.set(\"size\", options.size.toString());\n\n    const response = await this.makeRequest(`/media?${params}`);\n    return response.json();\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**What's happening here:**\n\n1. **OAuth Authentication**: We use `Authorization: Bearer ${token}`\n2. **Error Handling**: Structured error parsing with fallbacks\n3. **Flexibility**: Support for filtering by type, status, variants, and pagination\n4. **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript interfaces for all API responses\n\n### Building the Photo Grid Component\n\nNow let's create a React component that displays user images in a responsive grid. This component demonstrates pagination, loading states, and error handling patterns you'll use throughout the vault.\n\n```typescript\n'use client';\n\n// components/PhotoGrid.tsx\nimport { useState, const [images, setImages] = useState\u003cMediaItem[]\u003e([]);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n  const [error, setError] = useState\u003cstring | null\u003e(null);\n  const [page, else {\n        setLoadingMore(true);\n      }\n\n      // 📡 Fetch user's images with specific filtering\n      const response = await api.getUserMedia({\n        mediaType: 'image',          // Only images, not videos\n        variants: ['thumbnail_gallery'], // Optimized thumbnails for grid display\n        status: ['ready'],           // Only fully processed images\n        page: pageNum,\n \u0026\u0026 hasThumbnail;\n      });\n\n      setImages(prev =\u003e reset ? vaultImages : [...prev, ...vaultImages]);\n      setHasMore(response.pagination.hasMore);\n      setError(null);\n    } {\n    if (refreshTrigger \u0026\u0026 refreshTrigger \u003e 0) {\n      loadImages(1, true);\n      setPage(1);\n    }\n  }, [refreshTrigger, loadImages]);\n\n  const loadMore = useCallback(() hasMore, loading, page, loadImages]);\n\n  // 🎨 Loading state for initial load\n  if (loading \u0026\u0026 images.length === 0) {\n    return (\n      \u003cdiv className=\"flex items-center justify-center p-12\"\u003e\n        \u003cdiv className=\"text-center\"\u003e\n          \u003cdiv className=\"inline-block animate-spin rounded-full h-8 w-8 border-b-2 border-blue-600 mb-4\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"text-gray-600\"\u003eLoading your vault...\u003c/p\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n    );\n  }\n\n  // ❌ Error state with retry option\n  if (error \u0026\u0026 images.length === 0) {\n    return (\n      \u003cdiv className=\"text-center p-12\"\u003e\n        \u003cdiv className=\"text-red-600 mb-4\"\u003e\n strokeWidth={2} d=\"M12 8v4m0 4h.01M21 12a9 9 0 11-18 0 9 9 0 0118 0z\" /\u003e\n          \u003c/svg\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"text-lg font-medium\"\u003eFailed to load images\u003c/p\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"text-sm mt-2 text-gray-600\"\u003e{error}\u003c/p\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003cbutton\n          onClick={() =\u003e loadImages(1,    );\n  }\n\n  // 📭 Empty state when user has no images\n  if (images.length === 0 \u0026\u0026 !loading) {\n    return (\n      \u003cdiv className=\"text-center p-12\"\u003e\n        \u003cdiv strokeLinecap=\"round\" strokeLinejoin=\"round\" strokeWidth={1} d=\"M4 16l4.586-4.586a2 2 0 012.828 0L16 16m-2-2l1.586-1.586a2 2 0 012.828 0L20 14m-6-6h.01M6 20h12a2 2 0 002-2V6a2 2 0 00-2-2H6a2 2 0 image grid\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"w-full\"\u003e\n      {/* Responsive grid that adapts to screen size */}\n      \u003cdiv className=\"grid grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-5 xl:grid-cols-6 gap-2 sm:gap-3 md:gap-4\"\u003e\n        {images.map((item) =\u003e {\n          const thumbnail = item.variants?.find(v =\u003e v.variantType === \u003cdiv className=\"absolute inset-0 bg-black bg-opacity-0 group-hover:bg-opacity-30 transition-all duration-200 flex items-end\"\u003e\n                    \u003cdiv className=\"p-2 text-white \u003cp className=\"text-xs\"\u003eNo preview\u003c/p\u003e\n                  \u003c/div\u003e\n                \u003c/div\u003e\n              )}\n            \u003c/div\u003e\n          );\n        })}\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n\n      {/* Loading indicator for \"Load More\" */}\n      {loadingMore \u0026\u0026 (\n        \u003cdiv className=\"text-center p-8\"\u003e\n          \u003cdiv Optimized with `useCallback` to prevent unnecessary re-renders\n\nNow that you understand how to display existing images, let's move on to the exciting part: implementing multipart upload!\n\n## Step 2: Implement Multipart Upload\n\nHere's where we get to the core of this tutorial. We'll build a complete multipart upload system that handles large files efficiently, provides real-time progress feedback, and gracefully handles errors.\n\n### Understanding the Upload Flow (Again, but with Code)\n\nBefore we dive into implementation, let's revisit the 3-phase flow with more technical detail:\n\n**Phase 1: Initiate Upload**\n\n* Client tells server: \"I want to upload a 50MB image called 'vacation.jpg'\"\n* Server creates a database record for the media\n* Server tells cloud storage: \"Prepare for a multipart upload\"\n* Server returns: `{mediaUuid: \"abc-123\", uploadId: \"xyz-789\"}`\n\n**Phase 2: Upload Parts**\n\n* Client splits file into 10MB chunks (5 chunks for our 50MB file)\n* For each chunk:\n  * Client asks: \"Where do I upload chunk 3?\"\n  * Server returns a signed URL (valid for \\~15 minutes)\n  * Client uploads chunk directly to cloud storage\n  * Storage returns an ETag (proof of successful upload)\n\n**Phase 3: Finalize Upload**\n\n* Client tells server: \"All chunks uploaded, here are the ETags\"\n* Server tells storage: \"Combine all chunks into final file\"\n* Storage combines chunks and confirms success\n* Server starts processing pipeline (thumbnail generation, etc.)\n\n### Enhanced API Client with Upload Methods\n\nLet's extend our API client to support the multipart upload flow:\n\n```typescript\n// lib/fanvue-api.ts (continued)\n// Add these methods to the existing FanvueAPI class\n\nexport interface UploadPart {\n  ETag?: string;\n  PartNumber: number;\n}\n\nexport interface InitiateUploadResponse {\n  mediaUuid: string;\n  uploadId: string;\n}\n\n// Add these methods to your existing FanvueAPI class:\n\n  // 🚀 Phase 1: Create upload session\n  async createUploadSession(file: File): Promise\u003cInitiateUploadResponse\u003e {\n    const response = await this.makeRequest('/media/uploads', {\n      method: 'POST',\n      body: JSON.stringify({\n        name: file.name,          // Display name for the media\n        filename: file.name,      // Original filename\n        mediaType: 'image',       // We're focusing on images\n      }),\n    });\n\n    return response.json();\n  }\n\n  // 📍 Phase 2a: Get signed URL for specific chunk\n  async getPartUrl(\n    uploadId: string,\n    partNumber: number\n  ): Promise\u003cstring\u003e {\n    const response = await this.makeRequest(`/media/uploads/${uploadId}/parts/${partNumber}/url`, {\n      method: 'GET',\n    });\n\n    // Response is plain text (the signed URL)\n    return response.text();\n  }\n\n  // 📦 Phase 2b: Upload chunk to signed URL\n  async uploadPart(signedUrl: string, chunk: Blob): Promise\u003cstring\u003e {\n    // Note: This request goes directly to cloud storage, not our API\n    const response = await fetch(signedUrl, {\n      method: 'PUT',\n      body: chunk,\n      headers: {\n        'Content-Type': 'application/octet-stream',\n      },\n      // Don't add Authorization header - signed URL handles auth\n    });\n\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      throw new Error(`Part upload failed: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);\n    }\n\n    // Cloud storage returns ETag header as proof of successful upload\n    const etag = response.headers.get('ETag');\n    if (!etag) {\n      throw new Error('No ETag received from part upload');\n    }\n\n    return etag;\n  }\n\n  // ✅ Phase 3: Complete upload session\n  async completeUploadSession(\n    uploadId: string,\n    parts: UploadPart[]\n  ): Promise\u003c{ status: string }\u003e {\n    const response = await this.makeRequest(`/media/uploads/${uploadId}`, {\n      method: 'PATCH',\n      body: JSON.stringify({\n        parts, // Array of {ETag, PartNumber} objects\n      }),\n    });\n\n    return response.json();\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**What's happening in these upload methods:**\n\n1. **createUploadSession**: Creates media record and starts multipart session\n2. **getPartUrl**: Gets signed URL for uploading a specific chunk using RESTful URL structure\n3. **uploadPart**: Uploads chunk directly to cloud storage (bypasses our API)\n4. **completeUploadSession**: Tells server to combine all chunks into final file using PATCH method\n\n### Error Handling for Upload Operations\n\nNetwork operations can fail in many ways, especially with large file uploads. Let's add robust error handling:\n\n```typescript\n// lib/upload-errors.ts\n// Structured error handling for upload operations\n\nexport class UploadError extends Error {\n  constructor(\n    message: string,\n    public code: string,\n    public retryable: boolean = false,\n    public originalError?: unknown\n  ) {\n    super(message);\n    this.name = \"UploadError\";\n  }\n}\n\nexport function handleApiError(error: any): UploadError {\n  // Handle fetch abort (user cancelled)\n  if (error.name === \"AbortError\") {\n    return new UploadError(\"Upload cancelled by user\", \"CANCELLED\");\n  }\n\n  const message = error.message || \"Unknown error occurred\";\n\n  // File too large\n  if (message.includes(\"413\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"too large\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\"File is too large. Maximum size is 1GB.\", \"FILE_TOO_LARGE\");\n  }\n\n  // Rate limiting (temporary issue)\n  if (message.includes(\"429\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"rate limit\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\n      \"Too many requests. Please wait a moment before retrying.\",\n      \"RATE_LIMITED\",\n      true // This is retryable\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Authentication issues\n  if (message.includes(\"401\") || message.includes(\"403\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\"Authentication failed. Please log in again.\", \"AUTHENTICATION_FAILED\");\n  }\n\n  // Server errors (likely temporary)\n  if (message.includes(\"5\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"server error\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\n      \"Server error occurred. Please try again.\",\n      \"SERVER_ERROR\",\n      true // Server errors are usually temporary\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Generic network error (likely temporary)\n  if (message.toLowerCase().includes(\"network\") || message.toLowerCase().includes(\"fetch\")) {\n    return new UploadError(\n      \"Network error. Please check your connection and try again.\",\n      \"NETWORK_ERROR\",\n      true\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Unknown error\n  return new UploadError(\n    message,\n    \"UNKNOWN_ERROR\",\n    true // When in doubt, allow retry\n  );\n}\n```\n\n### Building the Upload Progress Component\n\nBefore we build the main uploader, let's create a component that shows upload progress. This provides immediate user feedback and makes the upload feel responsive:\n\n```typescript\n// components/UploadProgress.tsx\n// Visual progress indicator for individual file uploads\n\ninterface UploadProgressProps {\n  fileName: string;\n  progress: number; // 0-100\n  status: 'uploading' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'error';\n  error?: string;\n  onRetry?: () =\u003e void;\n  onCancel?: () =\u003e void;\n}\n\nexport function UploadProgress({\n  fileName,\n  progress,\n  status,\n  error,\n  onRetry,\n  onCancel\n}: UploadProgressProps) {\n  // 📝 Dynamic text based on current status\n  const getStatusText = () =\u003e {\n    switch (status) {\n      case 'uploading':\n        return `Uploading... ${Math.round(progress)}%`;\n      case 'processing':\n        return 'Processing...'; // Server is creating thumbnails, etc.\n      case 'completed':\n        return 'Upload complete!';\n      case 'error':\n        return 'Upload failed';\n      default:\n        return 'bg-red-600';\n      default:\n        return 'bg-gray-400';\n    }\n  };\n\n  // 🔄 Icon for current status\n  const getIcon = () =\u003e {\n    switch (status) {\n      case 'uploading':\n        return (\n          \u003csvg className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" fill=\"none\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\u003e\n            \u003ccircle\n              className=\"opacity-25\"\n              cx=\"12\"\n rounded-full transition-all duration-300 ease-out ${getStatusColor()}`}\n          style={{\n            width: `${status === 'error' ? 100 : Math.max(progress, 2)}%`\n          }}\n        /\u003e\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n\n      {/* Error message */}\n      {status === 'error' \u0026\u0026 error \u0026\u0026 (\n        \u003cdiv className=\"mt-2 p-2 bg-red-50 border border-red-200 rounded text-xs text-red-800\"\u003e\n          \u003cdiv className=\"flex items-start space-x-2\"\u003e\n            \u003csvg className=\"w-4 h-4 text-red-500 flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5\" fill=\"currentColor\" viewBox=\"0 0 20 20\"\u003e\n              \u003cpath fillRule=\"evenodd\" d=\"M18 10a8 8 0 11-16 0 8 8 0 0116 0zm-7 4a1 1 0 11-2 0 1 1 0 012 0zm-1-9a1 1 0 00-1 1v4a1 1 0 102 0V6a1 1 0 00-1-1z\" clipRule=\"evenodd\" /\u003e\n            \u003c/svg\u003e\n            \u003cp className=\"flex-1\"\u003e{error}\u003c/p\u003e\n          \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n      )}\n\n      {/* Processing explanation */}\n      {status === 'processing' \u0026\u0026 (\n        \u003cdiv className=\"mt-2 p-2 bg-yellow-50 border border-yellow-200 rounded text-xs text-yellow-800\"\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"flex items-center space-x-2\"\u003e\n            \u003csvg className=\"w-4 h-4 text-yellow-500 animate-pulse\" fill=\"currentColor\" viewBox=\"0 0 20 20\"\u003e\n              \u003cpath fillRule=\"evenodd\" d=\"M18 10a8 8 0 11-16 0 8 8 0 0116 0zm-7-4a1 1 0 11-2 0 1 1 0 012 0zM9 9a1 1 0 000 2v3a1 1 0 001 1h1a1 1 0 100-2v-3a1 1 0 00-1-1H9z\" clipRule=\"evenodd\" /\u003e\n            \u003c/svg\u003e\n            \u003cspan\u003eCreating thumbnails and optimizing your image...\u003c/span\u003e\n          \u003c/p\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n      )}\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\nThis progress component handles all the visual feedback during upload. The key features are:\n\n1. **Status-based styling**: Different colors and icons for each state\n2. **Progress calculation**: Smooth progress bar with minimum 2% width for visibility\n3. **Interactive elements**: Cancel and retry buttons when appropriate\n4. **Educational messaging**: Explains what \"processing\" means to users\n\n### Building the Main Upload Component\n\nNow comes the centerpiece: a React component that handles the complete multipart upload flow. This component coordinates file chunking, parallel uploads, progress tracking, and error recovery.\n\n```typescript\n'use client';\n\n// components/ImageUploader.tsx\n// Complete multipart upload implementation with queue management\n\nimport { useState, useCallback, useRef } from 'react';\nimport { FanvueAPI, UploadPart, handleApiError, UploadError } from '../lib/fanvue-api';\nimport { UploadProgress } from './UploadProgress';\n\n// Configuration constants\nconst CHUNK_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB chunks - good balance of reliability and performance\nconst MAX_CONCURRENT_UPLOADS = 3;    // Limit concurrent uploads to avoid overwhelming the server\nconst MAX_FILE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024 * 1024; // 1GB limit\nconst MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS = 3;        // Auto-retry failed chunks up to 3 times\n\ninterface UploadTask {\n  id: string;\n  file: File;\n  progress: number;\n  status: 'uploading' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'error';\n  error?: string;\n  mediaUuid?: string;\n  uploadId?: string;\n  abortController?: AbortController;\n  retryCount: number;\n}\n\ninterface ImageUploaderProps {\n  accessToken: string; // 🔑 OAuth token\n  onUploadComplete?: () =\u003e void;\n  maxFiles?: number;\n  acceptedTypes?: string[];\n  disabled?: boolean;\n}\n\nexport function ImageUploader({\n  accessToken,\n  onUploadComplete,\n  maxFiles = 10,\n  acceptedTypes = ['image/*'],\n  disabled = false\n}: ImageUploaderProps) {\n  const [uploads, setUploads] = useState\u003cUploadTask[]\u003e([]);\n  const fileInputRef = useRef\u003cHTMLInputElement\u003e(null);\n\n  // Queue management for concurrent uploads\n  const uploadQueueRef = useRef\u003c(() =\u003e Promise\u003cvoid\u003e)[]\u003e([]);\n  const runningUploads = useRef(0);\n\n  const api = new FanvueAPI(accessToken);\n\n  // 🔄 Helper to update upload state\n  const updateUpload = useCallback((id: string, updates: Partial\u003cUploadTask\u003e) =\u003e {\n    setUploads(prev =\u003e prev.map(upload =\u003e\n      upload.id === id ? { ...upload, ...updates } : upload\n    ));\n  }, []);\n\n  const removeUpload = useCallback((id: string) =\u003e {\n    setUploads(prev =\u003e prev.filter(upload =\u003e upload.id !== id));\n  }, []);\n\n  // 🎯 Queue management - processes uploads with concurrency limit\n  const processUploadQueue = useCallback(async () =\u003e {\n    if (runningUploads.current \u003e= MAX_CONCURRENT_UPLOADS || uploadQueueRef.current.length === 0) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const uploadTask = uploadQueueRef.current.shift();\n    if (uploadTask) {\n      runningUploads.current++;\n      try {\n        await uploadTask();\n      } finally {\n        runningUploads.current--;\n        // Process next item in queue\n        setTimeout(processUploadQueue, 100);\n      }\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  // 🚀 Main upload function - implements the 3-phase multipart flow\n  const uploadFile = useCallback(async (file: File): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e =\u003e {\n    const uploadId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`;\n    const abortController = new AbortController();\n\n    const upload: UploadTask = {\n      id: uploadId,\n      file,\n      progress: 0,\n      status: 'uploading',\n      abortController,\n      retryCount: 0,\n    };\n\n    setUploads(prev =\u003e [...prev, upload]);\n\n    try {\n      // 🔍 Phase 1: Create upload session\n      if (abortController.signal.aborted) throw new Error('Upload cancelled');\n\n      const initResponse = await api.createUploadSession(file);\n      updateUpload(uploadId, {\n        mediaUuid: initResponse.mediaUuid,\n        uploadId: initResponse.uploadId,\n      });\n\n      // 📦 Phase 2: Upload parts in parallel\n      const chunks = Math.ceil(file.size / CHUNK_SIZE);\n      const parts: Array\u003c{ ETag: string; PartNumber: number }\u003e = new Array(chunks);\n      const uploadPromises: Promise\u003cvoid\u003e[] = [];\n\n      // Create upload promises for each chunk\n      for (let partNumber = 1; partNumber \u003c= chunks; partNumber++) {\n        const uploadPartPromise = async () =\u003e {\n          if (abortController.signal.aborted) throw new Error('Upload cancelled');\n\n          const start = (partNumber - 1) * CHUNK_SIZE;\n          const end = Math.min(start + CHUNK_SIZE, file.size);\n          const chunk = file.slice(start, end);\n\n          try {\n            // Get signed URL for this specific chunk\n            const partUrl = await api.getPartUrl(\n              initResponse.uploadId,\n              partNumber\n            );\n\n            // Upload chunk directly to cloud storage\n            const etag = await api.uploadPart(partUrl, chunk);\n            parts[partNumber - 1] = { ETag: etag, PartNumber: partNumber };\n\n            // Update progress (thread-safe because we're using the part number as index)\n            const completedChunks = parts.filter(Boolean).length;\n            const progress = (completedChunks / chunks) * 100;\n            updateUpload(uploadId, { progress });\n\n          } catch (error) {\n            throw new Error(`Failed to upload chunk ${partNumber}: ${error}`);\n          }\n        };\n\n        uploadPromises.push(uploadPartPromise());\n\n        // ⚡ Process chunks in batches to avoid overwhelming the browser\n        if (uploadPromises.length \u003e= 3) {\n          await Promise.all(uploadPromises);\n          uploadPromises.length = 0;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Wait for any remaining uploads\n      if (uploadPromises.length \u003e 0) {\n        await Promise.all(uploadPromises);\n      }\n\n      // Verify all parts uploaded successfully\n      if (parts.length !== chunks || parts.some(part =\u003e !part)) {\n        throw new Error('Some chunks failed to upload');\n      }\n\n      // ✅ Phase 3: Complete upload session\n      updateUpload(uploadId, { status: 'processing', progress: 100 });\n\n      await api.completeUploadSession(\n        initResponse.uploadId,\n        parts\n      );\n\n      updateUpload(uploadId, {\n        status: 'completed',\n        progress: 100\n      });\n\n      // Notify parent component\n      onUploadComplete?.();\n\n    } catch (error) {\n      const uploadError = handleApiError(error);\n      updateUpload(uploadId, {\n        status: 'error',\n        error: uploadError.message,\n      });\n\n      // Auto-retry for retryable errors (rate limits, network issues, etc.)\n      if (uploadError.retryable \u0026\u0026 upload.retryCount \u003c MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS) {\n        setTimeout(() =\u003e retryUpload(uploadId), 2000 * (upload.retryCount + 1));\n      }\n    }\n  }, [accessToken, onUploadComplete, api, updateUpload]);\n\n  // 📋 Queue upload with concurrency management\n  const queueUpload = useCallback((file: File) =\u003e {\n    const uploadTask = () =\u003e uploadFile(file);\n    uploadQueueRef.current.push(uploadTask);\n    processUploadQueue();\n  }, [uploadFile, processUploadQueue]);\n\n  // ✅ File validation before upload\n  const validateFile = useCallback((file: File): string | null =\u003e {\n    // Check file type\n    const isValidType = acceptedTypes.some(type =\u003e {\n      if (type === 'image/*') return file.type.startsWith('image/');\n      return file.type === type;\n    });\n\n    if (!isValidType) {\n      return 'File type not supported. Please select an image file.';\n    }\n\n    // Check file size\n    if (file.size \u003e MAX_FILE_SIZE) {\n      return `File too large. Maximum size is ${MAX_FILE_SIZE / (1024 * 1024)}MB.`;\n    }\n\n    if (file.size === 0) {\n      return 'File is empty.';\n    }\n\n    return null;\n  }, [acceptedTypes]);\n\n  // 📁 Handle file selection from input\n  const handleFileSelect = useCallback((files: FileList | null) =\u003e {\n    if (!files || disabled) return;\n\n    const fileArray = Array.from(files);\n    const currentUploads = uploads.length;\n\n    // Check file limits\n    if (currentUploads + fileArray.length \u003e maxFiles) {\n      alert(`Maximum ${maxFiles} files allowed. You can upload ${maxFiles - currentUploads} more files.`);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Validate and queue each file\n    fileArray.forEach(file =\u003e {\n      const error = validateFile(file);\n      if (error) {\n        alert(`${file.name}: ${error}`);\n        return;\n      }\n\n      queueUpload(file);\n    });\n\n    // Reset input for re-selection of same files\n    if (fileInputRef.current) {\n      fileInputRef.current.value = '';\n    }\n  }, [disabled, uploads.length, maxFiles, validateFile, queueUpload]);\n\n  // 🔄 Retry failed upload\n  const retryUpload = useCallback((uploadId: string) =\u003e {\n    const upload = uploads.find(u =\u003e u.id === uploadId);\n    if (!upload) return;\n\n    // Cancel any existing upload\n    upload.abortController?.abort();\n\n    // Reset state and increment retry count\n    updateUpload(uploadId, {\n      status: 'uploading',\n      progress: 0,\n      error: undefined,\n      retryCount: upload.retryCount + 1,\n      abortController: new AbortController(),\n    });\n\n    // Queue the retry\n    queueUpload(upload.file);\n  }, [uploads, updateUpload, queueUpload]);\n\n  // ❌ Cancel upload\n  const cancelUpload = useCallback((uploadId: string) =\u003e {\n    const upload = uploads.find(u =\u003e u.id === uploadId);\n    if (upload) {\n      upload.abortController?.abort();\n      removeUpload(uploadId);\n    }\n  }, [uploads, removeUpload]);\n\n  // 🧹 Clear completed uploads\n  const clearCompleted = useCallback(() =\u003e {\n    setUploads(prev =\u003e prev.filter(upload =\u003e upload.status !== 'completed'));\n  }, []);\n\n  const completedCount = uploads.filter(u =\u003e u.status === 'completed').length;\n  const hasUploads = uploads.length \u003e 0;\n\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"w-full\"\u003e\n      {/* File Selection Area */}\n      \u003cdiv\n        className={`relative border-2 border-dashed rounded-lg p-8 text-center transition-colors ${\n          disabled\n            ? 'border-gray-200 bg-gray-50 cursor-not-allowed'\n            : 'border-gray-300 hover:border-blue-400 hover:bg-blue-50 cursor-pointer'\n        }`}\n        onClick={() =\u003e !disabled \u0026\u0026 fileInputRef.current?.click()}\n      \u003e\n        \u003cinput\n          ref={fileInputRef}\n          type=\"file\"\n          multiple\n          accept={acceptedTypes.join(',')}\n          onChange={(e) =\u003e handleFileSelect(e.target.files)}\n          className=\"hidden\"\n          disabled={disabled}\n        /\u003e\n\n        \u003cdiv className={`${disabled ? 'text-gray-400' : 'text-gray-600'}`}\u003e\n          \u003csvg\n            className=\"mx-auto h-12 w-12 mb-4\"\n            stroke=\"currentColor\"\n            fill=\"none\"\n            viewBox=\"0 0 48 48\"\n            aria-hidden=\"true\"\n          \u003e\n            \u003cpath\n              d=\"M28 8H12a4 4 0 00-4 4v20m32-12v8m0 0v8a4 4 0 01-4 4H12a4 4 0 01-4-4v-4m32-4l-3.172-3.172a4 4 0 00-5.656 0L28 28M8 32l9.172-9.172a4 4 0 015.656 0L28 28m0 0l4 4m4-24h8m-4-4v8m-12 4h.02\"\n              strokeWidth={2}\n              strokeLinecap=\"round\"\n              strokeLinejoin=\"round\"\n            /\u003e\n          \u003c/svg\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"text-lg font-medium\"\u003e\n            {disabled ? 'Upload disabled' : 'Upload images to your vault'}\n          \u003c/p\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"text-sm mt-1\"\u003e\n            {disabled\n              ? 'Please authenticate to upload files'\n              : 'Click to select files'\n            }\n          \u003c/p\u003e\n          \u003cp className=\"text-xs mt-2 text-gray-500\"\u003e\n            Max {maxFiles} files, up to {MAX_FILE_SIZE / (1024 * 1024)}MB each\n          \u003c/p\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n\n      {/* Upload List */}\n      {hasUploads \u0026\u0026 (\n        \u003cdiv className=\"mt-6 space-y-3\"\u003e\n          \u003cdiv className=\"flex items-center justify-between\"\u003e\n            \u003ch3 className=\"text-lg font-medium\"\u003e\n              Uploads ({uploads.length})\n            \u003c/h3\u003e\n            {completedCount \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 (\n              \u003cbutton\n                onClick={clearCompleted}\n                className=\"text-sm text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 underline\"\n              \u003e\n                Clear completed ({completedCount})\n              \u003c/button\u003e\n            )}\n          \u003c/div\u003e\n\n          \u003cdiv className=\"space-y-3 max-h-96 overflow-y-auto\"\u003e\n            {uploads.map(upload =\u003e (\n              \u003cUploadProgress\n                key={upload.id}\n                fileName={upload.file.name}\n                progress={upload.progress}\n                status={upload.status}\n                error={upload.error}\n                onRetry={() =\u003e retryUpload(upload.id)}\n                onCancel={\n                  upload.status === 'uploading'\n                    ? () =\u003e cancelUpload(upload.id)\n                    : undefined\n                }\n              /\u003e\n            ))}\n          \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n      )}\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\n**Key Implementation Details:**\n\n1. **Chunking Strategy**: Files are split into 10MB chunks for optimal balance of reliability and performance\n2. **Concurrency Control**: Maximum 3 concurrent uploads to avoid overwhelming the server\n3. **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress updates as chunks complete\n4. **Error Recovery**: Automatic retry for network issues and rate limits\n5. **User Control**: Users can cancel uploads and retry failed ones\n6. **Queue Management**: Multiple files are queued and processed efficiently\n\n### Understanding the Upload Logic\n\nLet's break down the complex upload logic step by step:\n\n**File Validation**\n\n* Checks file type matches accepted formats\n* Ensures file size is within limits\n* Provides clear error messages for invalid files\n\n**Upload Orchestration**\n\n1. **Initiate**: Create media record and get upload session ID\n2. **Chunk Processing**: Split file and upload chunks in parallel batches\n3. **Progress Tracking**: Update UI as each chunk completes\n4. **Finalization**: Combine chunks server-side and start processing\n\n**Error Handling Strategy**\n\n* **Network Errors**: Auto-retry with exponential backoff\n* **Rate Limits**: Wait and retry automatically\n* **File Errors**: Show user-friendly messages\n* **Cancellation**: Clean up resources properly\n\nThis completes our multipart upload implementation! The component handles all the complexity of chunking, uploading, and error recovery while providing a clean interface for users.\n\n## Step 3: Bringing It All Together\n\nNow let's combine our PhotoGrid and ImageUploader components into a complete vault page that demonstrates the full user experience.\n\n### Creating the Vault Page\n\nHere's how all our components work together in a manage your images with advanced multipart upload technology\n          \u003c/p\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n\n        \u003cSuspense fallback={\n          \u003cdiv className=\"flex items-center justify-center p-12\"\u003e\n            \u003cdiv className=\"animate-spin rounded-full h-8 w-8 border-b-2 border-blue-600\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n          \u003c/div\u003e\n        }\u003e\n          \u003cVaultContent /\u003e\n        \u003c/Suspense\u003e\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\n### The Main Vault Component\n\n```typescript\n'use client';\n\n// \u003cul className=\"text-xs text-blue-800 space-y-1\"\u003e\n              \u003cli\u003e• POST /uploads - Create upload session\u003c/li\u003e\n              \u003cli\u003e• GET
